reviews.review_description: This app is glitchy as everything and you can't really do much this game needs to be polished and upgraded in order for it to work so I will put 3 🌟s until the whole thing is fixed up good enough
More level please...
This is a horrid game. I downloaded it hoping for a good vr game. I CANT PLAY. Because the dumb cabinet wont spawn right and i cant fix it.
Love the concept and execution; gave 4 stars only due to bugs. I would gladly pay for more levels.
But it does not load at all . Shame.
I wish there was more and I wish we could create our own escape room.
I really enjoyed this game, the only downfall is it was to short.
I tried to play but it gets to the analyzing room sign and won't do anything else

reviews.review_title: No AR game should ever have boundaries.
reviews.review_description: Simple rule: if it uses environment passthrough, boundaries should go off. Why do i want an annoying blue virtual cage interrupting the experience with absolutely no use or upside in AR

First, for the pp on quest 3 that have issues with the stupid boundaries.
1) need to put quest 3 on dev mod
2) creat stupid boundaries 
3) Start the game
4) don t close the game, just go in "oculus" menu and desactivat boundaries in developers' settings.
5) go back to your game and enjoy.

Ps: didn't test how fare you could walk then, but improved the distance for me and no boundaries shows up wenn close of my walls. Work in a 10m by 5m room

The game now:
Was really impressed how good all the  virtual stuff fit in my room and blended with the real things.
But had a lot of issues with objets falling through the floor and disappeared, even for my first start with the paper falling down. Had to restart to get passt the intro that open the cupboard. Sometimes if I let fall objects, they stay on the floor and sometimes they fall through and disappear completely from the game. So, I had to restart often.  A good solution would be an option to reste the object to the initial place after 1min if he his no more reachable.
Edit: tried with quest 2 and had no issue with objects going through the floor. The Problem was only on quest3. 
Multiplayer works with 1quest2 and 1quest 3. We were able to finish the game. But some bugs appear.  The 2nd player could not see that the drawer was open and I could open it again-> the key then was floating in the air 50cm of the drawer. Some objects are not in the same place for both players.  Wenn we finished the game every objects duplicate and we had 2 rooms in one. 


The game needs to be polished but has a lot of potential. Had a lot of fun and the level of the puzzles was perfect.

reviews.review_title: Great game but multiplayer is a bugged
reviews.review_description:
https://youtu.be/ufOnd5gYFCk

I love this game, the puzzles are a bit too difficult sometimes but the technique behind this game is perfect... for a soloplayer game. Playing this in multiplayer is lots of fun, but there are bugs. Only the host really sees things as they are. For instance, if the host takes the key from the clock, the other players still see the key inside the clock. Anything done by a joined player might or might not work (the bucket may hang too low and fall, but in the eyes of that player it's allright. It has to be right in the eyes of the hostplayer). If this bug could be fixed, the game would be PERFECT.

you developers in agreement with meta are creating more and more apps where it is mandatory to have the connection open even if you are in singleplayer, even if the app does not require a connection to work, you force players to have the connection open because then meta stores the game data, which is valuable data that he perhaps resells.

this and many other apps that are coming out can be started and run even without an open connection, but you are forced to keep it open, I don't like this drift that the meta platform is taking at all, and I will give you a star every time I find one similar app that requires an open connection to launch the game in single player mode

what you are doing takes away freedom from users, because the day there is no connection or there is a problem with the servers we will have the games on the viewer but without the "premise" of the network we cannot start even for a single player!

p.s. Before the writer I know that you warn in the game sheet that the internet connection is mandatory for this app but the matter does not change! I have been using the Quest since the first model and this change, especially for those who do not have a good connection or live in places where the connection is poor and sometimes absent, is not good at all, especially when the app could work easily without an open connection.

This is supposed to be a first-party app that shows off the magic and wonder of Quest 3 passthrough and semantic segmentation, but it's a horrible mess. It's completely ignorant of where you've set your boundary, and initially places objects WAY outside my boundary (often inside walls). It lets you reposition objects, but it's very limited (some objects can only be repositioned in very restrictive ways, while others can't be repositioned at all) and even after several minutes of wrestling with the repositioning system there were still numerous objects that were outside my boundary, which caused the game to pause and go invisible if I tried to interact with them. It was literally impossible for me to do the most basic tasks like placing a bucket to collect sand because the sand stream was just outside of my boundary. And whereas every single other AR / VR game where you have to interact with things outside your reach gives you some sort of "long distance grab" (see the "I Expect You To Die" series for a great example), this does not and requires you to directly touch everything, while most things are outside of your bounds. And the salt in the wound is that there's no way to reposition objects once you've started the game, so if something is JUST out of reach you can't just nudge it over, and instead have to start over from scratch and fight with the positioning system again.

This was obviously designed and tested in a big empty open-floorplan office, but nobody thought about how it might work in an actual home with furniture in it.

And on top of that, the interactions are awful; at the very beginning of the game you have to unscrew a lightbulb, and no matter what motion you make with your hands the lightbulb just twists back and forth about 10 degrees.

Huge misstep for a launch title, with what could have been a dazzling killer app buried under a slew of careless design mistakes.

reviews.review_title: Troubling to get to work, but very fun
reviews.review_description:
With an hour of effort I understand how to to make it work. 
First you have to have developer setting enabled so you can disable the  boundary. But you have to have the boundary enabled when you start or else the letter will fall through the ground. 
In some cases other important things also fall through the ground. You have to restart in those instances. 

I'm glad they are working on making more MR content at least

So far so good, but with a few downsides.
1. You can't save the game with the room setup. I really hate to setup the whole room again if I don't finish the game.
2. I take off the quest 3 , put it back on, and most of the AR  gadgets  are gone.
3. I can't finish the game cause gadgets disappear from time to time. 

But, it really looks amazing. Can't wait for an update..

I really enjoyed this. Reminds me of the Room VR A Dark Matter. It's a pity it is only one level, but it looks good and is pretty challenging. 
There were a few graphical glitches, and the floor would not calibrate properly, making the paper difficult to find and pick up.

This app was pretty finicky. It took a few tries to properly map out the room so objects weren’t in walls, outside boundaries, or under furniture.

A few times objects disappeared leaving the other unavailable.

That being said, I really liked the bones for this game. The puzzle and atmosphere were both really engaging. 

I was legitimately disappointed when we finished the level. I assumed the next set of cabinets were going to open but instead we got some credits and it was over.

Hoping to see more to this, or maybe another game similar. I know there are other puzzle games like this but I haven’t found any that also have multiplayer.

Having an awful time playing with a friend, with colocation failing and putting their name tag in the wrong spot (and putting the objects in the wrong spot because of it) but it’s a fun idea and hope to see more! Haven’t been able to complete it yet, possibly due to buggy location, but I’m hopeful this can become more accurate in the future, and maybe even expand upon  the game, making more levels, etc.

Very Nice and entertainen Puzzle Game, Reminds me of the 7th Guest, but in AR.
I have not solved it, but the first steps looks promising! As other people remarked, you have to calibrate the room and shuffle cubes where the objects will appear. In some cases, things were slightly outside the boundary. I have an empty 4x4 meter room, otherwise it might be though to get a playable set-up.
It is not a full game, more of a demo, but the first decent attempt at AR that I have seen.
Look forward for a complete release/experience. Please make a complete game/more levels of this.

reviews.review_title: Take My Money! Or Not. Free Is Me.
reviews.review_description:
As a sucker for good mixed reality, I love this demo. Looking forward to full release. 

Great job, developer people. Now what I do with this darn key? What am I missing?

reviews.review_title: An example how to NOT make puzzle games.
reviews.review_description:
Terrible font in writings. Almost illegible for non-natives. Too low resolution even on quest 3.
The object placement algorithm ignores furniture.
Grabbibg objects by hand is done very ugly.

reviews.review_title: Meta store your data and sell it!!
reviews.review_description: Within this app ,meta stores your data and sells it .So,if you have dignity,don't download this app.
I just love tgis game. It has technical issues, but it is still amazing. also would loce to have more escape room games like this!
Ideas like this are great. Keep it up

I love it.  Still has a few rough edges, don't get me wrong.  You have to make sure you place the cubes where they are not in the red before you start or you may have adverse affects.  And a few synchronization issues my first time through but I love where this is heading. 

A couple of main issues that I'm assuming the devs are aware of without giving any spoilers away. 

The bucket is sporadic unless placed cautiously. 

Light bulb is a bit tricky

We have up on the key until we went back and revisited it then when placed in the exact chief spot it worked. 

Still extremely fun.  Thank you!
Could not get the app to spatially anchor between two Quest 3 headsets in the same room. Instead objects were appearing in different locations for each player.

reviews.review_title: It’s great, looking forward to more
reviews.review_description:
This is a great little escape game.
Looks like it could be customisable to make many levels.
Really enjoyed this, well done 👏

Absolutely fantastic! Tested so far multiplayer between Quest 2 and Quest 3. Super novel to have an escape room in your living room. Mechanics and controls are certainly workable enough. Noticed some minor and very occasional quirks with moving objects; did not get in the way of the fun!
Some of these reviews are a bit out of control; like they've paid 50 bucks for this FREE experience.

Boundary literally ruins this game. It's an AR game, I can see everything, that's the whole point of AR... Why do I need a giant mesh that shows me where objects and walls are WHEN I CAN ALREADY SEE THEM WITH MY OWN EYES! Absolutely ruins the immersion. Literally makes zero sense.

Installed it.  Once finished with the opening screen nothing happens
Doesn't work

Although not unique in terms of EscapetheRoom in Real Life, it is an incremental Revolution! Way more possibilities for illusions, riddles etc. than in real life!! Can't wait for another game like this.

Most importantly for developer: I would pay for this kind of game with a few more rooms! ;-]

Doesn’t work well. Room scan doesn’t handle vaulted ceiling while first encounter does. 
Worst is that it insists on placing furniture outside my boundary where I can’t get to it. 
Unplayable. 
Menu continually moves to the left if you try to look at the left side of the menu. Same with right side. 
Unusable.
Items in walls i can't get to , multiplayer most the time the keyboard wont show to enter room code , shame i know it is app lab but shoukd be better at this point.
